headline: Quantum Frontiers: From Hardware to Holography
summary: Today's research spans the entire quantum stack, from hardware innovations that dramatically accelerate atom array assembly to deep theoretical insights into thermodynamics, topological symmetries, and the mathematical stability of spectral gaps.

themes.title: Quantum Hardware & Control
themes.body: Researchers have developed an FPGA-based electronic control system that bypasses PC-loop latencies, cutting feedback time to 282 microseconds and boosting the preparation of defect-free 10-atom arrays to a 95.4% success rate.

themes.title: Theoretical Foundations & Holography
themes.body: New theoretical work challenges assumptions in quantum gravity and mathematical physics, showing that subregion complementarity in finite-N CFTs can exist without error-correcting codes, and that the undecidability of 1D spectral gaps is highly unstable.

themes.title: Dynamics, Symmetries & Thermodynamics
themes.body: Advances in quantum many-body systems introduce a new thermodynamic framework for out-of-equilibrium temperatures, a method to extract conformal data from real-time Loschmidt echoes, and a symmetry-based route to generalized bosonic Kitaev chains.

connections.body: Both papers explore the boundary physics of quantum systems, with one extracting conformal field theory data from real-time dynamics and the other analyzing subregion reconstruction in holographic CFTs.
